name: Transalpine Gaulish
notes: Transalpine Gaulish (6th c. BCE – 5th c. CE). ISO 639-3: xtg. Continental Celtic language of Gaul (modern France, Belgium, Switzerland). ~800+ inscriptions including Larzac, Chamalières tablets and Coligny calendar. THE key substrate for French and Gallo-Romance. Distinctive 'tau gallicum' /ts/ < *t (fronting). Nasal vowels attested. Celtic p-loss. Lenition well-developed. Vigesimal counting inherited by French (quatre-vingts). ~200 words in French (Lambert 2003, Delamarre 2003). Written in Greek (south), Latin (north), and Lugano alphabets.
